cards.back: The graph showing the speed of adoption of a new technology or process, often in the form of a S-curve, with low adoption at the start and increasing adoption over time
An iterative and incremental approach to software development, emphasizing collaboration, flexibility, and rapid delivery
The expenses incurred by organizations due to changes in processes, technologies, or market conditions, which can be significant and long-lasting
A visual system for managing work, emphasizing continuous flow and limiting work in progress, often used in Agile environments
The cost of implementing quick fixes or workarounds in software development, which can lead to technical issues and maintenance problems in the future
